archetype: Pastel & Soft / Jewel Hybrid (Adani One Style)

welcome_card.layout: Row with text on left and 3D illustration or large icon on right

layout_patterns.login_screen: Clean white background, university logo centered, Input fields with subtle border, solid primary color button.
layout_patterns.role_select: List of 3 large cards (Student, Staff, Admin). Cards have distinct icons and subtle drop shadows.
layout_patterns.home_dashboard: Scrollable View. Header (Logo, User info, What's New, Notification icon) -> Welcome Card -> Section Header (Academics) -> Grid of Service Tiles (3 or 4 columns) -> Promotional Banner -> Section Header (Campus Life) -> Grid.
layout_patterns.module_screens: Back button in header, Large title, Tab pills for sub-navigation, content mostly in cards.
layout_patterns.sos_emergency: Floating Action Button (FAB) anchored bottom-right or top-right in header across app except Auth screens.

promotional_banners.description: Happy university students socializing on campus, perfect for campus life banner.
Students walking past historic building, perfect for welcome or admission banners.

animation_patterns.button_press: Scale down to 0.95 with ease out
animation_patterns.modals: Bottom sheet slide up for quick actions (Visitor QR, Hall Ticket preview)
animation_patterns.alerts: Subtle bounce on notification icon if new

instructions_to_main_agent: Use @expo/vector-icons for all service tile icons. The design relies on realistic large icons with a subtle background circle.
Ensure all interactive and key informational elements include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., data-testid='login-button', data-testid='service-tile-library').
Implement the layout precisely using the defined 8pt spacing scale.
Follow the React Native safe area context to avoid overlaps with the notch/status bar.
Use react-native-reanimated for the SOS pulse animation.
The Home dashboard should be scrollable and modular so different roles (Student, Staff) see different grids.
